summary: PQ-Aliro makes genuine engineering contributions with byte-accurate overhead analysis and open-source prototype. However two critical deficiencies block acceptance: §7 is entirely missing, and android:isolatedProcess is set to 'false' in the manifest contradicting the key security claim.
strengths: First byte-accurate APDU-level overhead analysis for PQC NFC protocol
Formal security model competently adapted from BR and Lyu et al.
Open-source prototype covers Android HCE and PC Reader
Background Java Thread for signing to avoid ANR deadlock is a useful engineering insight
weaknesses: CRITICAL: AndroidManifest.xml sets android:isolatedProcess=false but paper claims true—security claim factually wrong
MAJOR: Formal transcript H(pk_e||c_e) diverges from actual code which hashes full TLV AUTH0 bytes including UD_noise and reader_identifier
MAJOR: Latency model omits per-APDU round-trip time (FWT)—estimate may be 2-3x optimistic. Thread.sleep(500) adds 500ms per signing poll not mentioned
MAJOR: testFullProtocol() runs AUTH0→AUTH1→LOADCERT not AUTH0→LOADCERT→AUTH1 as specified
MINOR: 'background coroutine' should be 'background Java Thread'
MINOR: BER-TLV overhead description ambiguous (3 vs 4 bytes, conflates tag byte with length bytes)
MINOR: ECC baseline 1.2KB is theoretical not measured—ECC path partially unimplemented

detailed_comments.comment: Security Proof section contains only one sentence. §7 heading at line 893 then immediately §8.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Provide complete proof or at minimum rigorous proof sketch with theorem statement and reductions to ML-KEM/ML-DSA hardness

detailed_comments.comment: Paper claims android:isolatedProcess='true' but AndroidManifest.xml line 57 sets isolatedProcess='false'. Named process (:pqc_signer) ≠ isolated process. Isolated process has distinct UID, no filesystem access. The paper's security argument does not hold.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Either fix manifest to isolatedProcess=true (requires ContentProvider for SharedPreferences access) or correct paper to describe named-process isolation and its actual security properties

detailed_comments.comment: Formal transcript=H(pk_e||c_e) but actual transAH0=SHAKE256-512(full TLV AUTH0_cmd||AUTH0_resp) including UD_noise, reader_identifier, transaction_identifier. Formal model and implementation are misaligned.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Update formal protocol to match implementation or vice versa; explicitly address UD_noise in key derivation

detailed_comments.comment: Latency model = bytes/bit_rate only. Omits per-APDU FWT round-trip. Thread.sleep(500) in getNextChunkWith61() adds 500ms per signing poll—significant unmentioned latency source.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Add round-trip overhead term citing ISO/IEC 14443-4 FWT values; document the 500ms polling interval and its impact

detailed_comments.comment: 'background coroutine' in paper but actual code uses raw Java Thread (MyHostApduService.java:163 new Thread(()->{})).
200B/240B chunk sizes are implementation choices not ISO/IEC 14443 constraints. ISO-DEP I-block INF field max is 254 bytes (not 255—PCB byte included in 255).
detailed_comments.suggestion: Clarify these are conservative implementation limits; cite ISO/IEC 14443-4 directly

code_discrepancies.paper_claim: dispatching ML-DSA signing to background coroutine
32-byte shared secret immediately imported to Keystore then zeroed
code_discrepancies.actual_code: UserDevice.java:322 calls generateAndSecureSession() helper; zeroing is inside PQKeyManager, not shown directly

missing_citations.claim: 424 kbit/s typically negotiated by Android HCE
missing_citations.suggested_reference_type: Empirical study or Android HCE documentation
Separate citation for Dilithium signing on Android ARM (Lima21 cites Kyber KEM, not Dilithium signing)—e.g., pqm4 or Kannwischer et al.
Legion of the Bouncy Castle official citation
